title: Remove quick links & contact section, update background/colors, add warranty info

requirements.summary: Remove the Quick Links section from the Footer component while keeping brand info, contact details, and operating hours
acceptanceCriteria: The footer no longer contains a 'Quick Links' or navigation links column
Brand info, phone, email, address, and hours remain visible in the footer
No broken layout or empty columns appear where quick links used to be

file_operations.description: Remove the entire Quick Links section (the navigation links column with smooth-scroll anchors). Restructure the footer grid layout to a two-column or single-column layout that preserves the brand info and contact details (phone, email, address, operating hours). Ensure no empty grid cells or broken spacing remains after removal.

requirements.summary: Remove the Contact section component from the page entirely
acceptanceCriteria: The Contact section with phone cards and email link no longer appears on the page
No visual gap or blank area remains where the Contact section was
App.tsx no longer imports or renders the Contact component

file_operations.description: Remove the import statement for the Contact component and remove its usage from the component render tree. Verify that no spacing or layout issues remain after the component is removed from the page composition.

requirements.summary: Update the website's background and color scheme to use a warm golden-amber and deep charcoal palette with solar energy accents
acceptanceCriteria: The primary color tokens are updated to warm amber/golden tones
The background uses a deep charcoal or dark slate color instead of the previous theme
Accent and CTA buttons use a bright orange-yellow tone
All sections visually reflect the new color palette consistently
Text remains legible against new backgrounds with sufficient contrast

file_operations.description: Replace the existing OKLCH color tokens for both light and dark themes to reflect the new warm golden-amber and deep charcoal palette. Update --background to use deep charcoal/slate tones (e.g., oklch(0.15 0.02 260)), --primary to warm amber/golden tones (e.g., oklch(0.70 0.15 70)), and --accent to bright orange-yellow (e.g., oklch(0.75 0.20 50)). Update foreground, muted, card, border, and other semantic tokens to maintain sufficient contrast and legibility against the new backgrounds. Preserve existing utility classes for gradients and animations.

Update the custom color palette extensions (solar, forest, and semantic UI colors) to align with the new warm amber/golden and charcoal theme. Replace solar color values with warm amber/golden oklch tones for primary shades, update background colors to deep charcoal/slate, and set accent colors to bright orange-yellow. Ensure all extended colors use oklch format and maintain sufficient contrast ratios for accessibility.

requirements.summary: Add a Warranty & Guarantee section displaying 25-Year Solar Panel Warranty, 10-Year Inverter Warranty, and 5-Year Free Service Guarantee
acceptanceCriteria: A new Warranty & Guarantee section is visible on the page
25-Year Warranty on Solar Panels is displayed with an icon
10-Year Warranty on Inverter is displayed with an icon
5-Year Free Service Guarantee by Vendor is displayed with an icon
The section is styled consistently with the updated site color theme
The section is responsive and displays correctly on mobile and desktop

file_operations.description: Create a new Warranty & Guarantee section component that displays three warranty/guarantee items in a responsive grid: (1) 25-Year Warranty on Solar Panels, (2) 10-Year Warranty on Inverter, (3) 5-Year Free Service Guarantee by Vendor. Use shield or badge icons (from lucide-react) for each item. Style the section consistently with the new warm amber/charcoal color theme defined in index.css and tailwind.config.js, using card backgrounds, hover effects, and responsive layouts (single column on mobile, three columns on desktop). Include a section heading and descriptive text for each warranty item.

Import the new WarrantyGuarantee component and insert it into the page composition after the WhyChooseUs section and before the Services section. Ensure the component is rendered in the correct order within the main App component's return statement.
